channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. says it seized an Iranian cargo ship, ceasefire expires soon
item.description: The two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire Wednesday. And the two sides seem far from a resolution. President Trump says the US has taken custody of an Iranian vessel.
item.content:encoded: The two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire Wednesday. And the two sides seem far from a resolution. President Trump says the US has taken custody of an Iranian vessel.
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: At the Canton Fair, Chinese manufacturing takes center stage
item.description: This past week the Chinese city of Guangzhou has been hosting the Canton Fair, the biggest trade show in China. It showcases products manufactured in China.
item.content:encoded: This past week the Chinese city of Guangzhou has been hosting the Canton Fair, the biggest trade show in China. It showcases products manufactured in China.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: In Virginia, redistricting vote could give Democrats an edge ahead of midterms
item.description: Voting ends Tuesday in Virginia on a ballot measure that would lead to a new congressional map for the state. These new districts could provide Democrats a big boost ahead of the midterms.
item.content:encoded: Voting ends Tuesday in Virginia on a ballot measure that would lead to a new congressional map for the state. These new districts could provide Democrats a big boost ahead of the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Will the DHS shutdown affect security for the World Cup?
item.description: The FIFA World Cup is a little over 50 days away. NPR's Rob Schmitz talks to former Department of Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem about the security concerns people have about hosting the tournament in America in this moment.
item.content:encoded: The FIFA World Cup is a little over 50 days away. NPR's Rob Schmitz talks to former Department of Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem about the security concerns people have about hosting the tournament in America in this moment.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: 50 years after "All The President's Men," do films still explore presidential power?
item.description: All the President's Men hit theaters in April 1976. Fifty years on, is Hollywood still making movies that explore the abuse of presidential power?
item.content:encoded: All the President's Men hit theaters in April 1976. Fifty years on, is Hollywood still making movies that explore the abuse of presidential power?
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: "Classical Music Happy Hour" podcast shows a different side of classical music
item.description: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with pianist Emanuel Ax about his new podcast "Classical Music Happy Hour."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with pianist Emanuel Ax about his new podcast "Classical Music Happy Hour."
item.dc:creator: Mallory Yu

item.title: 8 children killed in a shooting in Louisiana, police say
item.description: The suspect was the father of seven of the children killed, police said. The victims ranged in age from 1 to about 14 years old, according to police in Shreveport, La. A total of 10 people were shot.
item.content:encoded:
The suspect was the father of seven of the children killed, police said. The victims ranged in age from 1 to about 14 years old, according to police in Shreveport, La. A total of 10 people were shot.

(Image credit: Gerald Herbert)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again, Israel-Lebanon ceasefire tenuously holds
item.description: Iran's military says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again, while a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon is strained, but still in place.
item.content:encoded: Iran's military says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again, while a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon is strained, but still in place.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Tensions between President Trump and Pope Leo put American Catholics in awkward spot
item.description: The divide between the White House and the Vatican is widening after President Trump sharply criticized Pope Leo and the leader of the Catholic Church said he doesn't fear Trump.
item.content:encoded: The divide between the White House and the Vatican is widening after President Trump sharply criticized Pope Leo and the leader of the Catholic Church said he doesn't fear Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jack Morgan

item.title: Newly approved mining in Minnesota may threaten waterways of a beloved nature preserve
item.description: Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness in Minnesota is filled with more than a thousand lakes and rivers. But a recent vote in congress to allow mining nearby could threaten the preserve's waterways.
item.content:encoded: Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness in Minnesota is filled with more than a thousand lakes and rivers. But a recent vote in congress to allow mining nearby could threaten the preserve's waterways.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson
